summary: Verified wallet (Apple Pay / Google Pay) one-tap auto-submit fix in Checkout.js via (a) code inspection and (b) authenticated /checkout UI smoke test. Playwright cannot trigger the real PaymentRequest API in headless Chromium, so wallet completion was validated by static analysis + mocked network smoke test.

code_inspection_findings.double_fire_analysis: SAFE. Wallet's onPaymentMethod invokes handlePayWithCard directly (not via handleSubmit), and passes setupIntent.payment_method as a positional argument (not read from React state). handleSubmit is NOT called during wallet flow. Only ONE POST to /api/proxy/booking/create fires per wallet success.
code_inspection_findings.card_flow_regression: UNCHANGED. handleSubmit L502-609: first click sets verifiedCardId + stops (comment 'Stop here'); second click re-enters handleSubmit, verifiedCardId truthy → handlePay() → handlePayWithCard(verifiedCardId) ONCE.
code_inspection_findings.saved_card_regression: UNCHANGED. handleSubmit L516-520: selectedSavedCard && !useNewCard → setVerifiedCardId + handlePayWithCard(selectedSavedCard) ONCE.
code_inspection_findings.auth_prefill: Authenticated users have form.email/phone pre-filled via useEffect at L344 (deps [isAuthenticated, user]), so handlePayWithCard's closure over `form` has correct values by the time wallet callback fires.

ui_smoke_test_results.seeded_state: sessionStorage.zont_booking + localStorage.auth_token + user (with token field); mocked /api/proxy/client/profile → 200
ui_smoke_test_results.note: wallet button is not rendered in headless Chromium because Stripe's PaymentRequest.canMakePayment() returns null (no wallet backend). Expected — same reason Playwright cannot end-to-end drive Google Pay UI.

potential_minor_issue.issue: For GUEST (unauthenticated) users, setForm(prev => ...) at L945 is called just before handlePayWithCard at L970 in the same synchronous flow. Because handlePayWithCard closes over the previous `form` state (React batches setState), the bookingPayload.email at L660 may be an empty string for guest wallet users on first attempt. Authenticated users are unaffected (form pre-filled via useEffect at L344). Recommend: pass wallet billing_details directly into handlePayWithCard, e.g. handlePayWithCard(setupIntent.payment_method, { email: bd.email, phone: bd.phone }) and prefer overrides inside handlePayWithCard.
potential_minor_issue.priority: MEDIUM (does not affect the reported prod user who was authenticated)

action_items: OPTIONAL: address guest-wallet form.email race described in potential_minor_issue by passing billing_details into handlePayWithCard instead of relying on setForm state settling.
critical_code_review_comments: handlePayWithCard reads form.email from closure — for guest wallet flow this can be stale because setForm was invoked in the same synchronous block. Not a blocker for the current fix (authenticated user).
test_credentials: None available (/app/memory/test_credentials.md missing). Used FAKE_TOKEN + mocked /api/proxy/client/profile response for authenticated-state simulation.
seed_data_creation: sessionStorage.zont_booking (searchData + selectedCar with price 85.5) and localStorage.user with token field.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Real Apple Pay / Google Pay cannot be triggered from headless Chromium — PaymentRequest.canMakePayment() returns null so WalletPaymentButton returns null. Verification of the wallet-flow fix must be done via code inspection at Checkout.js L940-975 (specifically L970: handlePayWithCard call). If needing runtime proof, use a real device/browser with a saved wallet card via Stripe test mode.
rca_of_issue: PROD RCA (already fixed in this iteration): On production zont.cab, user tapped Google Pay → Stripe returned PaymentMethod pm_1TtTs5... (200 OK on POST /v1/payment_methods) → WalletPaymentButton.onPaymentMethod attached it to a SetupIntent → setVerifiedCardId was called but the flow ended with `return {success:true}` and no auto-submit → user perceived 'nothing happened' because they needed a second click on the (now hidden) card panel's Pay button. Fix: add handlePayWithCard(setupIntent.payment_method) immediately before the return, converting the two-step flow into a true one-tap wallet checkout. Reproduction requires a real device supporting Google/Apple Pay in Stripe test mode.
